title: Retrieve Appointment Preferences
description: Fetches the existing configuration for appointment preferences, including rules for deal creation, job sheet visibility, and mark as complete configurations.
purpose: Fetches the existing configuration for appointment preferences, including rules for deal creation, job sheet visibility, and mark as complete configurations.

parameter_details.name: include
parameter_details.description: Include additional related data in the response. For example, specify `deal_record_configuration` to include deal configuration details.

schema.description: A JSON object containing the appointment preferences configured in the CRM.

appointment_preferences.description: The complete set of appointment preferences configured in CRM.

show_job_sheet.description: Indicates whether job sheets are shown for appointments.

when_duration_exceeds.description: Defines how to handle appointments when the scheduled duration is exceeded.

when_appointment_completed.description: Determines whether a deal is automatically created when an appointment is completed.

allow_booking_outside_service_availabili.description: If true, allows booking appointments outside defined service availability.

allow_booking_outside_businesshours.description: If true, allows appointments to be scheduled beyond business hours.

deal_record_configuration.description: Configuration for creating deal records after an appointment is completed.

layout.description: Details of the layout used when creating deal records.

field_mappings.description: List of field mappings from appointment data to deal fields.

id.description: Unique identifier for the deal record configuration.

title: Update Appointment Preferences
description: Updates one appointment preferences in Zoho CRM. This endpoint allows configuring how appointments are handled, including deal creation.
purpose: Updates one appointment preferences in Zoho CRM. This endpoint allows configuring how appointments are handled, including deal creation.

schema.description: Request body containing appointment preference fields to update.

appointment_preferences.description: The appointment preference data to be updated.

show_job_sheet.description: Set to true to display job sheets for appointments.

when_duration_exceeds.description: Specifies how to handle appointments exceeding their duration.

when_appointment_completed.description: Specifies whether to create a deal automatically when an appointment is completed.

allow_booking_outside_service_availabili.description: If true, allows appointments to be booked outside service availability.

allow_booking_outside_businesshours.description: If true, allows appointments to be booked outside business hours.

deal_record_configuration.description: Defines how deal records should be created after an appointment.

layout.description: Defines which layout to use for the deal record.

id.description: Unique identifier of the layout (Zoho internal ID).

field_mappings.description: List of field mappings for deal creation.

items.description: A single field mapping object containing the target field, mapping type and mapped value.

schema.description: Response object returned after successful update, containing status metadata and details.

appointment_preferences.description: Response details confirming the update status.

status.description: Indicates if the request was successful.

code.description: Response code for a successful update.

details.description: Additional details regarding the updated preferences. May contain operation metadata.

400.description: Returned when mandatory parameters are missing or invalid data is provided.

schema.description: Error response for invalid or incomplete request data. Top-level contains an `appointment_preferences` object with error details.

code.description: Error code indicating a required field is missing.

message.description: Human-readable error message for missing mandatory field.

details.description: Details about which field is missing and its JSON path.

api_name.description: API name of the missing field.

json_path.description: JSON path pointing to the missing field in the request body.

title: Convert a Lead
description: Converts a Lead record into Contact, Account, and/or Deal records. Allows configuration of conversion behavior including overwrite settings, notifications, owner assignment, and tag carryover.
purpose: Converts a Lead record into Contact, Account, and/or Deal records. Allows configuration of conversion behavior including overwrite settings, notifications, owner assignment, and tag carryover.

notify_lead_owner.description: If true, sends email notification to the Lead owner about the conversion. Default is false.

notify_new_entity_owner.description: If true, notifies the owner(s) of newly created Contact, Account, or Deal records. Default is false.

add_to_existing_record.description: When true, associates the Lead with an existing Account or Contact instead of creating duplicates.

assign_to.description: Specifies the user to assign the converted records to. If omitted, records are assigned to the current Lead owner.

title: Update Duplicate Check Preference
description: Update Duplicate Check Preference API is used to update duplicate check settings for a specific module in the CRM system. It allows users to define how duplicate records are identified and managed based on field mappings between the current module and a mapped module, such as Contacts. This API helps maintain data integrity by preventing the creation of duplicate records during data entry or import processes.
purpose: Update Duplicate Check Preference API is used to update duplicate check settings for a specific module in the CRM system. It allows users to define how duplicate records are identified and managed based on field mappings between the current module and a mapped module, such as Contacts. This API helps maintain data integrity by preventing the creation of duplicate records during data entry or import processes.

title: Uploads a file to ZFS
description: Uploads a file to Zoho File System (ZFS) and returns the file metadata including the file ID which can be used to associate the file with records in Zoho CRM. The file is uploaded using multipart/form-data with a required 'file' field containing the binary data of the file to be uploaded. The response includes the file ID, name, status, and other details. Can upload 10 files in a single request by repeating the 'file' field. Maximum file size is 20 MB.
purpose: Uploads a file to Zoho File System (ZFS) and returns the file metadata including the file ID which can be used to associate the file with records in Zoho CRM. The file is uploaded using multipart/form-data with a required 'file' field containing the binary data of the file to be uploaded. The response includes the file ID, name, status, and other details. Can upload 10 files in a single request by repeating the 'file' field. Maximum file size is 20 MB.

parameter_details.name: inline
parameter_details.description: To upload inline images. Possible value: inline

schema.description: Multipart form data for uploading one or more files to Zoho File System

file.description: Upload one or more files using repeated 'file' keys.

items.description: Binary content of the file to be uploaded

200.description: Successfully uploaded one or more files to Zoho File System. Returns an array of upload results, one per file, containing the file ID for record association and upload status confirmation.

schema.description: Response schema for successful file upload to Zoho File System

data.description: Array of upload results, one object per successfully uploaded file. Order corresponds to the order of files in the multipart request.

items.description: Upload result for a single file

message.description: Human-readable success message confirming the upload

details.description: File metadata for the uploaded file, including the unique identifier for CRM record association

400.description: Bad Request - The file upload request is malformed or invalid. Returns `failure_in_attachment_handling` when the 'file' field is missing or contains no file data. Returns `INVALID_REQUEST` when request constraints are violated, such as exceeding the 10-file limit per request.

schema.description: Returned when the 'file' field is missing from the multipart/form-data request or when a 'file' field is present but contains no actual file data.

code.description: Indicates missing or empty 'file' field in the upload request

message.description: Human-readable error message prompting proper file upload
enum: Problem in uploading attachment. kindly upload the file properly

details.description: Additional error context. Typically empty for this error type.

415.description: Unsupported Media Type / File Size Exceeded - Returned when one or more uploaded files exceed the maximum allowed size of 20 MB per file. The API rejects the entire request if any single file violates this constraint.

schema.description: Returned when any file in the upload request exceeds the 20 MB size limit. The entire upload is rejected, even if other files are within the allowed size.

code.description: Indicates one or more files exceed the 20 MB maximum file size limit

message.description: Human-readable error message prompting file size verification
enum: Please check if the size of the file is in the correct range

details.description: Additional error context. Typically empty for this error type.

title: Retrieves a file from ZFS
description: Retrieves the binary content of a file stored in Zoho File System (ZFS) using its unique file ID. The file ID is provided as a required query parameter 'id'. If the file exists, the API returns the file's binary data along with appropriate headers indicating the MIME type and content disposition for download. If the file ID does not correspond to any file in ZFS, a 204 No Content response is returned. This endpoint is used to download files that have been previously uploaded to ZFS and associated with Zoho CRM records.
purpose: Retrieves the binary content of a file stored in Zoho File System (ZFS) using its unique file ID. The file ID is provided as a required query parameter 'id'. If the file exists, the API returns the file's binary data along with appropriate headers indicating the MIME type and content disposition for download. If the file ID does not correspond to any file in ZFS, a 204 No Content response is returned. This endpoint is used to download files that have been previously uploaded to ZFS and associated with Zoho CRM records.

parameter_details.name: id
parameter_details.description: Unique identifier of the file to retrieve

200.description: Successfully uploaded file to Zoho File System. Returns the uploaded file as binary content with appropriate headers indicating the file type and disposition.

schema.description: Binary content of the uploaded file.

204.description: No Content - Returned when the provided file ID does not match any file in the Zoho File System. This occurs when the 'id' parameter contains an invalid file identifier, an empty string, or references a file that has been deleted or never existed.

400.description: Bad Request - The file retrieval request is missing required parameters. Returns `REQUIRED_PARAM_MISSING` when the 'id' query parameter is not included in the request.

title: PUT /settings/fiscal_year
description: Only Admins can update Fiscal YearAPI DOC : https://learn.zoho.in/portal/zohocorp/manual/v3-apis-1/article/custom-fiscal-year-support
purpose: Only Admins can update Fiscal YearAPI DOC : https://learn.zoho.in/portal/zohocorp/manual/v3-apis-1/article/custom-fiscal-year-support

schema.description: Request body schema

calendar_type.description: calendar_type of the fiscal_year can only be configured with "gregorian" or "custom" which indicates standard or custom fiscal year.

display_based_on.description: Indicates whether the fiscal year label is based on the start month or end month

surplus_week.description: In Custom Fiscal Year, a surplus week is an extra week added to keep the fiscal calendar aligned with the actual calendar year.

year.description: year of the surplus week.Can configure only for current year and the following year only

start_month.description: start_month is the month when the fiscal year begins. It can be configured for standard fiscal year(calendar_type = 'gregorian').

interval_display_option.description: interval_display_option indicates the display format for which the periods should get displayed.

structure.description: Structure pattern for custom fiscal year configuration. It allows 7 structures 4-4-5, 4-5-4, 5-4-4 (quarter-based) and 3-3-3-4, 3-3-4-3, 3-4-3-3, 4-3-3-3 (year-based) -- describing how weeks are distributed across "periods" and "quarters".

start_date.description: It indicates the start_date for the custom fiscal year which can be configured only for the current year.

title: Get Related Records Count
description: Retrieves the count of related records for a specific parent record. Supports filtering by various criteria including approval status, conversion status, and custom field filters. This operation is useful for analytics and UI display purposes where you need to show counts without fetching the actual related records.
purpose: Retrieves the count of related records for a specific parent record. Supports filtering by various criteria including approval status, conversion status, and custom field filters. This operation is useful for analytics and UI display purposes where you need to show counts without fetching the actual related records.

parameter_details.name: moduleApiName
parameter_details.description: The API name of the CRM module containing the parent record. Common values include 'Contacts', 'Leads', 'Deals', 'Accounts', 'Services__s', etc.

parameter_details.name: recordId
parameter_details.description: The unique identifier of the parent record for which to count related records. This is typically a numeric ID or UUID depending on the CRM configuration.

request_body.description: Request payload containing an array of related list specifications. Each item specifies a related list to count and optional filtering parameters to apply before counting. Supports filtering by field values, approval status, conversion status, and record categories.

id.description: The unique identifier of the related list definition.

params.description: Optional filtering and selection parameters to apply before counting records.

approved.description: Filter to include only approved records when counting.

filters.description: Field-based filter criteria to apply before counting records.

converted.description: Filter to include only converted records when counting (applicable to Leads).

title: Get all global picklists
description: Fetches the list of global picklists available in the organization along with their details. pick_list_values, used_in_modules, and associated_fields_count can be included in the response based on the 'include' query parameter. A 204 is returned when no global picklists exist.
purpose: Fetches the list of global picklists available in the organization along with their details. pick_list_values, used_in_modules, and associated_fields_count can be included in the response based on the 'include' query parameter. A 204 is returned when no global picklists exist.

parameter_details.name: include
parameter_details.description: Comma-separated list of related resources to include. Allowed values: "used_in_modules", "pick_list_values", "associated_fields_count". Use CSV encoding (style=form, explode=false) for multiple values, e.g. include=used_in_modules,pick_list_values.

parameter_details.name: include_inner_details

title: Update a global picklist by id
description: Updates an existing global picklist identified by the path parameter \{id\}. You can update display_label, api_name, description, pick_list_values_sorted_lexically, and pick_list_values. Backend-generated properties (actual_label, customizable, modified_by, created_by, presence) cannot be updated. For pick_list_values, you can add new values, update existing values (using id), move values between used/unused (one at a time), or delete values (one at a time using _delete: true). Bulk operations for moving to unused or deletion are not allowed.

title: Delete a global picklist by id
description: Deletes a single global picklist identified by the path parameter \{id\}. This request is asynchronous: a 202 Accepted indicates deletion has been scheduled and the response contains a job_id in details. There is no status-check endpoint. A 400 Bad Request is returned for invalid id, deletion already scheduled, conversion in progress, association limits, or system-defined restrictions. When an error refers to a position in a multi-resource request/path, details.resource_path_index is provided.

title: Replace picklist values for a global picklist
description: Schedules or executes replacement of picklist values for the specified global picklist. You can specify either `id`, `display_value`, or both for `old_value` and `new_value`. If both are provided, the system validates them for consistency. The `from` (old) value can be non-existent in the current picklist, but the `to` (new) value must be in the **used** state of the picklist option.

title: Get all layouts metadata for a module
description:
Retrieves comprehensive details of all layouts associated with a specified module in your Zoho CRM account. Returns layout configuration including sections, fields, profiles, and permissions in a single response without pagination.

**Important Notes:**
- The `profiles` array will be `null` if the user does not have "Module Customization" permission in their profile.
- For Deals module: When the pipeline feature is enabled, multiple layouts exist per pipeline. Each pipeline can have its own set of layouts.
- The `mode` parameter supports different values based on module type. Common modes include `business_card` and `quick_create`.
- Score and Visit Summary sections are system-generated and read-only.
- All layouts for the module are returned in a single response; pagination is not supported.
purpose:
Retrieves comprehensive details of all layouts associated with a specified module in your Zoho CRM account. Returns layout configuration including sections, fields, profiles, and permissions in a single response without pagination.

**Important Notes:**
- The `profiles` array will be `null` if the user does not have "Module Customization" permission in their profile.
- For Deals module: When the pipeline feature is enabled, multiple layouts exist per pipeline. Each pipeline can have its own set of layouts.
- The `mode` parameter supports different values based on module type. Common modes include `business_card` and `quick_create`.
- Score and Visit Summary sections are system-generated and read-only.
- All layouts for the module are returned in a single response; pagination is not supported.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: Specify the API name of the required module. Supports both standard modules (Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Deals, Campaigns, Tasks, Cases, Events, Calls, Solutions, Products, Vendors, Price_Books, Quotes, Sales_Orders, Purchase_Orders, Invoices, Appointments, Services) and custom modules. Module API names are case-insensitive, can contain alphanumeric characters and underscores, must not contain spaces, and have a maximum length of 50 characters. Use the GET - Modules Metadata API to retrieve all available module API names.

parameter_details.name: mode
parameter_details.description:
Filter layouts by mode. Available modes vary by module type:
- `business_card`: Business card layout view
- `quick_create`: Quick create form layout
- `all`: Request all available modes

If not provided, the default mode for the module will be applied. Note: Mode availability depends on the module and its configuration.

title: Get a specific layout metadata by ID
description:
Retrieves comprehensive details of a specific layout by its unique identifier for a specified module in your Zoho CRM account. Returns layout configuration including sections, fields, profiles, and permissions.

**Important Notes:**
- The `profiles` array will be `null` if the user does not have "Module Customization" permission in their profile.
- For Deals module: When the pipeline feature is enabled, multiple layouts exist per pipeline. Each pipeline can have its own set of layouts.
- The `mode` parameter supports different values based on module type. Common modes include `business_card` and `quick_create`.
- Score and Visit Summary sections are system-generated and read-only.

**Prerequisite:** The Layout ID can be obtained from the Get All Layouts API.

title: Update a Layout
description: Update a custom layout in Zoho CRM. You can rename the layout, add/remove profile permissions, enable/disable business card, create/update/delete sections, and add/update/delete/move fields within sections. Limits: Maximum 5 sections per request and maximum 5 fields total across all sections per request.
purpose: Update a custom layout in Zoho CRM. You can rename the layout, add/remove profile permissions, enable/disable business card, create/update/delete sections, and add/update/delete/move fields within sections. Limits: Maximum 5 sections per request and maximum 5 fields total across all sections per request.

parameter_details.name: id
parameter_details.description: Unique identifier of the layout. Must be a valid 64-bit integer represented as a string. Can be obtained from the Get Layouts API.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: Specify the API name of the required module. Supports both standard modules (Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Deals, Campaigns, Tasks, Cases, Events, Calls, Solutions, Products, Vendors, Price_Books, Quotes, Sales_Orders, Purchase_Orders, Invoices, Appointments, Services) and custom modules. Module API names are case-insensitive, can contain alphanumeric characters and underscores, must not contain spaces, and have a maximum length of 50 characters. Use the GET - Modules Metadata API to retrieve all available module API names.

request_body.description: Layout update request body containing the layout modifications. Only a single layout can be updated per request. Maximum 5 sections and 5 fields (total across all sections) can be modified per request.

For updating an existing section, provide id along with properties to update. For deleting a section, provide id and _delete object.

title: Delete a custom layout
description: Deletes a custom layout from a module in Zoho CRM. When deleting a layout that has associated records, you must specify a `transfer_to` layout ID to transfer those records. If the layout has an associated pipeline, you must also provide `pipeline` and `stage` parameters. The `transfer_to` parameter is only optional when the layout is deactivated and has no records associated with it. Note: The standard layout cannot be deleted.
purpose: Deletes a custom layout from a module in Zoho CRM. When deleting a layout that has associated records, you must specify a `transfer_to` layout ID to transfer those records. If the layout has an associated pipeline, you must also provide `pipeline` and `stage` parameters. The `transfer_to` parameter is only optional when the layout is deactivated and has no records associated with it. Note: The standard layout cannot be deleted.

parameter_details.name: id
parameter_details.description: Unique identifier of the layout. Must be a valid 64-bit integer represented as a string. Can be obtained from the Get Layouts API.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: Specify the API name of the required module. Supports both standard modules (Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Deals, Campaigns, Tasks, Cases, Events, Calls, Solutions, Products, Vendors, Price_Books, Quotes, Sales_Orders, Purchase_Orders, Invoices, Appointments, Services) and custom modules. Module API names are case-insensitive, can contain alphanumeric characters and underscores, must not contain spaces, and have a maximum length of 50 characters. Use the GET - Modules Metadata API to retrieve all available module API names.

parameter_details.name: transfer_to
parameter_details.description: The ID of the layout to which records associated with the layout being deleted should be transferred. This parameter is conditionally mandatory - it is required when the layout has records associated with it. It is only optional when the layout is deactivated and has no records associated. The target layout must be an active layout; transferring to a deactivated layout is not allowed.

parameter_details.name: pipeline
parameter_details.description: The ID of the pipeline to transfer records to. This parameter is conditionally mandatory - it must be provided along with the `stage` parameter when the layout being deleted has an associated pipeline. Required for modules like Deals that support pipeline stages.

parameter_details.name: stage
parameter_details.description: The ID of the stage within the pipeline to transfer records to. This parameter is conditionally mandatory - it must be provided along with the `pipeline` parameter when the layout being deleted has an associated pipeline. The stage must be a valid stage within the specified pipeline.

200.description: Layout deleted successfully. The response contains the ID of the deleted layout.

schema.description: Response body for successful layout deletion.

layouts.description: Array containing the result of the layout deletion operation.

items.description: Result of a single layout deletion operation.

title: Activate layout with profile associations
description: Activates a single deactivated layout, making it available for use within the specified module. Optionally allows adding or removing profile associations during activation. Only one layout can be activated per request. This operation is idempotent - attempting to activate an already active layout returns an error (ALREADY_ACTIVATED).
purpose: Activates a single deactivated layout, making it available for use within the specified module. Optionally allows adding or removing profile associations during activation. Only one layout can be activated per request. This operation is idempotent - attempting to activate an already active layout returns an error (ALREADY_ACTIVATED).

parameter_details.name: id
parameter_details.description: The unique identifier of the layout to activate or deactivate. Must be a valid layout ID that exists within the specified module. Layout ID values are data center-specific.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: The CRM module API name that contains the layout. Supports both standard Zoho CRM modules (e.g., Leads, Contacts, Deals) and custom modules created in the organization. Module names follow PascalCase convention and may contain underscores for custom modules.

request_body.description: Request body for activating a single layout with optional profile association modifications. The layout can specify profile associations to add (default) or remove (using _delete flag). Profile modifications are optional - layout can be activated without any profile changes. At least one profile must remain associated with the layout after all operations are applied.

schema.description: Request payload for activating a single layout with optional profile associations. Only one layout can be activated per request.

layouts.description: Array containing exactly one layout object to activate. The layout includes its unique identifier and optionally the profiles to associate or disassociate. Profile associations can be added (default) or removed (using _delete flag). The array must contain exactly one item.

items.description: Layout activation configuration with optional profile associations

id.description: The unique identifier of the layout to activate. Must reference an existing deactivated layout within the specified module. The ID is represented as a string containing a 64-bit integer value. Layout IDs are data center-specific.

profiles.description: Optional array of profile associations for this layout. Profiles define which user roles can access this layout. Use the _delete flag to remove existing associations. Empty array or omitting this field entirely preserves existing profile associations. At least one profile must remain associated with the layout after all operations are applied. While duplicate profile IDs are technically allowed, they cause unnecessary processing and should be avoided.

items.description: Profile association configuration for the layout

200.description: Layout successfully activated. Returns confirmation with the activated layout ID and operation status.

schema.description: Success response for layout activation operation

layouts.description: Array containing the activation result for the layout

code.description: Operation result code indicating successful activation

details.description: Additional details about the activated layout

400.description: Bad request due to invalid parameters, layout constraints, or business rule violations

schema.description: Invalid layout ID or layout not found in the specified module

message.description: Error message describing the invalid data

resource_path_index.description: Index in the resource path where the error occurred

500.description: Internal server error occurred while processing the activation request

schema.description: Internal server error response

code.description: Error code for internal server errors

message.description: Error message describing the internal error

title: Deactivate layout with configuration transfer
description: Deactivates an active layout and transfers its configuration (profile associations, permissions, field mappings) to another active layout within the same module. At least one active layout must remain in the module. This operation is idempotent - attempting to deactivate an already deactivated layout returns an error (ALREADY_DEACTIVATED).
purpose: Deactivates an active layout and transfers its configuration (profile associations, permissions, field mappings) to another active layout within the same module. At least one active layout must remain in the module. This operation is idempotent - attempting to deactivate an already deactivated layout returns an error (ALREADY_DEACTIVATED).

parameter_details.name: id
parameter_details.description: The unique identifier of the layout to activate or deactivate. Must be a valid layout ID that exists within the specified module. Layout ID values are data center-specific.

title: Search Records by Criteria, Word, Email, or Phone
description: Searches records matching your criteria within a CRM module. Supports criteria queries, email/phone/word searches. At least one search parameter required. Max 2,000 records, 15 criteria conditions. Newly created records may have indexing delays.
purpose: Searches records matching your criteria within a CRM module. Supports criteria queries, email/phone/word searches. At least one search parameter required. Max 2,000 records, 15 criteria conditions. Newly created records may have indexing delays.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: The API name of the CRM module to search within.

parameter_details.name: approval_state
parameter_details.description: Filter records by their approval status. This parameter is particularly useful for modules with approval workflows or data validation processes.

parameter_details.name: criteria
parameter_details.description:
Complex query string to search for records using field-specific conditions and operators. Supports up to 15 conditions combined with AND/OR operators. **Format:** `(field_name:operator:value)` with logical operators.
*Mandatory if email, phone, and word are not present.*

parameter_details.name: converted
parameter_details.description: Filter records by their conversion status. Primarily used with Leads module to distinguish between converted and non-converted leads. Also applicable to other modules with conversion workflows.

parameter_details.name: word
parameter_details.description:
A simple word or phrase to search across all supported text fields in the module. Performs a global search with minimum 2 characters required. This is the broadest search method but may be slower than specific field searches.
*Mandatory if criteria, email, and phone are not present.*

parameter_details.name: email
parameter_details.description:
Search records by email address across all email fields in the module. Supports partial matches and searches multiple email fields simultaneously.
*Mandatory if criteria, phone, and word are not present.*

parameter_details.name: phone
parameter_details.description:
Search records by phone number across all phone fields in the module. Supports various phone number formats including international, national, and partial numbers.
*Mandatory if criteria, email, and word are not present.*

parameter_details.name: fields
parameter_details.description: Comma-separated list of field API names to retrieve in the response. If not specified, all accessible fields are returned. Use this to optimize response size and improve performance.

parameter_details.name: page
parameter_details.description: The page number to retrieve for paginated results. Use in combination with per_page to navigate through large result sets.

parameter_details.name: per_page
parameter_details.description: The number of records to return per page. Higher values reduce the number of API calls needed but increase response size and processing time.

parameter_details.name: sort_by
parameter_details.description: The field API name to sort the search results by. Choose a field that exists in the target module for consistent sorting behavior.

parameter_details.name: sort_order
parameter_details.description: The sorting direction for the results. Use 'asc' for ascending (A-Z, 1-9, oldest first) or 'desc' for descending (Z-A, 9-1, newest first).

parameter_details.name: type
parameter_details.description: Filter users by their status, role, or confirmation state

200.description: Successful response containing the list of records matching the search criteria.

title: Retrieve CRM module metadata
description: Fetches metadata for CRM modules including configuration, capabilities, and structural information. Supports filtering by feature name to retrieve modules associated with specific features, or by status (e.g., `system_hidden`) to return modules matching that status. When both `status` and `feature_name` are provided, modules must satisfy both criteria (AND logic). Expected latency: ~60ms.
purpose: Fetches metadata for CRM modules including configuration, capabilities, and structural information. Supports filtering by feature name to retrieve modules associated with specific features, or by status (e.g., `system_hidden`) to return modules matching that status. When both `status` and `feature_name` are provided, modules must satisfy both criteria (AND logic). Expected latency: ~60ms.

parameter_details.name: status
parameter_details.description: Filter modules by their visibility status. Accepts comma-separated values to retrieve modules matching any of the specified statuses. Case-sensitive.

parameter_details.name: feature_name
parameter_details.description: Filter modules by feature name. Retrieves modules associated with the specified feature. Case-sensitive, follows snake_case format. Empty string returns 400 error.

parameter_details.name: include
parameter_details.description: Comma-separated list of additional fields to include in the response. Allows retrieving optional module metadata that is not returned by default.

200.description: Successfully retrieved module metadata. Returns an array of module objects containing configuration details, permissions, fields, layouts, and related lists for each module. No pagination is applied; all matching modules are returned in a single response.

schema.description: Response containing an array of module metadata objects with complete configuration details.

modules.description: Array of module metadata objects, each containing complete configuration including fields, layouts, profiles, and permissions.

items.description: Complete metadata for a single CRM module including configuration, capabilities, fields, layouts, profiles, and related lists.

global_search_supported.description: Whether the module supports global search functionality.

public_fields_configured.description: Whether public fields have been configured for this module.

recycle_bin_on_delete.description: Whether deleted records are moved to the recycle bin instead of being permanently deleted.

has_more_profiles.description: Whether more profiles exist beyond those currently loaded (pagination indicator).

sub_menu_available.description: Whether a submenu is available for this module in the navigation.

lookupable.description: Whether the module supports lookup functionality and can be used as a lookup target in other modules.

profile_count.description: Number of profiles associated with this module. 0 indicates no profile assignments.

module_type.description: Type classification of the module. Only exposed for pathfinder and orchestration modules.

cc_enabled.description: Whether CommandCenter functionality is enabled for email communications. Only exposed for orchestration module (not API-supported).

deletable.description: Whether records in this module can be deleted by users.

description.description: User-defined description of the module's purpose.

creatable.description: Whether new records can be created in this module.

inventory_template_supported.description: Whether inventory templates are supported for this module.

modified_time.description: Timestamp when the module was last modified (timezone-aware).

presence_sub_menu.description: Whether the module has a presence submenu option.

triggers_supported.description: Whether workflow triggers are supported for this module.

api_name.description: API identifier for the module; follows naming convention (starts with letter, alphanumeric and underscores only).

plural_label.description: Plural display label for the module; if translation is enabled, contains the translated label, otherwise matches actual_plural_label.

actual_plural_label.description: Original plural label as stored in the database, without translation applied.

actual_singular_label.description: Original singular label as stored in the database, without translation applied.

singular_label.description: Singular display label for the module; if translation is enabled, contains the translated label, otherwise matches actual_singular_label.

isBlueprintSupported.description: Whether the module supports Blueprint (process automation) functionality.

visibility.description: Bitwise feature visibility flags; base value 1 indicates fully visible. Add sums of flags to hide module features: 2=WEBUIHIDDEN, 4=TABMENU, 8=LOOKUP, 16=RELATEDLIST, 32=MULTISELECTLOOKUP, 64=MODULE_SETTING, 128=AUTOMATION, 256=TEMPLATES, 512=IMPORT, 1024=EXPORT, 4096=REPORT, 8192=API. Example: 4612 (4+512+4096) hides TabMenu, Import and Report.

convertable.description: Whether records in this module can be converted to other module types.

editable.description: Whether records in this module can be edited by users.

emailTemplate_support.description: Whether email template functionality is supported for this module.

email_parser_supported.description: Whether email parsing capabilities are supported for this module.

filter_supported.description: Whether filtering functionality is available for records in this module.

show_as_tab.description: Whether the module appears as a tab in the main navigation.

web_link.description: URL for web tab modules; supports parameter substitution via arguments array.

viewable.description: Whether records in this module can be viewed by users.

api_supported.description: Whether API operations are allowed for this module.

quick_create.description: Whether quick create form is available for this module.

static_subform_properties.description: Configuration properties for static subform modules.

feeds_required.description: Whether activity feeds are required for this module.

scoring_supported.description: Whether lead/record scoring functionality is supported for this module.

webform_supported.description: Whether web form integration is supported for this module.

arguments.description: Array of argument objects used to substitute parameters in web tab URLs (e.g. https://...&id=${Users.User Id}&name=${Users.First Name}). Duplicates are allowed.

business_card_field_limit.description: Maximum number of business card fields allowed (non-negative).

access_type.description: Access control model for the module.

private_profile.description: Private profile configuration for the module.

track_current_data.description: Whether current data tracking is enabled for this module.

modified_by.description: User who last modified this custom view. Null for system views that have never been modified.

profiles.description: List of profiles associated with this module. May be empty for certain module types.

parent_module.description: Parent module reference if this is a child/related module. Empty object {} for standalone modules, null in some cases, or populated object with api_name and id for child modules.

status.description: Lifecycle/state of the module (e.g. visible to end users, hidden by system/user, or pending deletion). This status is orthogonal to the numeric bitwise feature flags in 'visibility'; it does not itself enable/disable individual features.

sequence_number.description: Display order of the module in the navigation/module list.

204.description: No content - returned when no modules match the specified filter criteria.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid parameter values. Returned when `status` contains unsupported values or when `feature_name` is provided as an empty string.

schema.description: Returned when status parameter contains invalid values

code.description: Error code indicating invalid data in the request.

details.description: Details about the validation error including which parameter failed and valid values.

index.description: Zero-based index of the invalid value in comma-separated status parameter

supported_values.description: Unordered list of valid status values. Each value appears exactly once.

items.description: A valid status value accepted by the API.

param_name.description: Name of the parameter that contains the invalid value

message.description: Human-readable error message describing the validation failure

status.description: Error status indicator. Always 'error' for failed requests.

title: Create a custom CRM module
description: Creates a single custom module in the CRM. Requires the Crm_Implied_Customize_Zoho_CRM permission. This operation is not idempotent; submitting the same api_name multiple times will result in validation errors. Only one module can be created per request; batch creation is not supported.
purpose: Creates a single custom module in the CRM. Requires the Crm_Implied_Customize_Zoho_CRM permission. This operation is not idempotent; submitting the same api_name multiple times will result in validation errors. Only one module can be created per request; batch creation is not supported.

request_body.description: Request body for creating custom modules in the CRM. Only single module creation is supported per request.

schema.description: Request body for creating a new custom module in Zoho CRM

modules.description: Array containing a single module definition to create. Only one module can be created per request.

items.description: Module definition object containing module metadata, profiles, and optional display field configuration.

singular_label.description: Singular form of the module name. Alphanumeric only, no special characters allowed.

plural_label.description: Plural form of the module name. Alphanumeric only, no special characters allowed.

api_name.description: Unique API identifier for the module. Alphanumeric only, no special characters allowed. Must be unique across all modules.

profiles.description: Array of profile IDs that determine which user profiles can access this module. Must contain at least one profile. Duplicate profile IDs are not allowed.

display_field.description: Defines the primary name field for the module. Optional, but if provided, either field_label or data_type is mandatory. If data_type is 'autonumber', then auto_number configuration with start_number is mandatory.

field_label.description: Label for the name field. Mandatory if display_field is provided and data_type is not specified.

data_type.description: Type of the name field. 'autonumber' for auto-generated sequential IDs (requires auto_number configuration), 'text' for user-entered text. Mandatory if display_field is provided and field_label is not specified.

auto_number.description: Configuration for auto-number generation. Mandatory when data_type is 'autonumber'.

201.description: Successfully created one or more custom modules. Returns the module ID for each created module.

schema.description: Success response containing the created module details

modules.description: Array containing the result of the single module creation request. Always contains exactly one item.

code.description: Operation status code indicating successful creation

details.description: Contains the unique identifier of the newly created module

message.description: Human-readable success message confirming module creation

400.description: Bad request - validation failure. Returned when input data fails validation (invalid module properties, duplicate api_name, missing required fields, field length exceeded, unsupported operations, or mandatory fields not found). All modules in the batch must be valid; no partial success occurs.

schema.description: Batch error format wrapping individual module validation errors in modules array

modules.description: Array of error responses for each module that failed validation

items.description: DUPLICATE_DATA error for duplicate api_name or other unique constraint violations

code.description: Error code indicating duplicate data constraint violation

details.description: Error details with field location information

message.description: Human-readable error message describing the duplicate data violation

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid OAuth scope. The OAuth token does not have the required scope to access this endpoint.

schema.description: OAUTH_SCOPE_MISMATCH error when OAuth scope is insufficient

code.description: Error code indicating OAuth scope mismatch

details.description: Additional context about the scope issue

403.description: Forbidden - insufficient permissions. User lacks required permissions to create custom modules.

schema.description: NO_PERMISSION error when user lacks required CRM customization permission

permissions.description: List of required permissions that the user lacks

title: Update CRM modules
description: Updates existing modules in the CRM. Allows modification of module labels and profile assignments. This operation is idempotent; the same request can be safely repeated. Supports batch updates - multiple modules can be updated in a single request. Returns 200 when all modules update successfully, or 207 Multi-Status when some succeed and others fail.
purpose: Updates existing modules in the CRM. Allows modification of module labels and profile assignments. This operation is idempotent; the same request can be safely repeated. Supports batch updates - multiple modules can be updated in a single request. Returns 200 when all modules update successfully, or 207 Multi-Status when some succeed and others fail.

request_body.description: Request body for updating modules. Supports batch updates - multiple modules can be updated in a single request. Partial updates are supported: only `id` is required; `singular_label`, `plural_label`, and `profiles` are optional and can be updated independently. Profile updates use delta semantics: profiles without `_delete: null` are added (if not already present), profiles with `_delete: null` are removed. Batch updates may result in partial success (207 Multi-Status response) if some modules succeed and others fail.

schema.description: Batch module update request containing an array of modules to update. Supports partial updates where only specified fields are modified.

modules.description: Array of modules to update. Supports batch updates with multiple modules in a single request. Each module must have a unique ID within the request.

items.description: Module update definition containing labels, module ID, and accessible profiles.

singular_label.description: Singular display label for the module (e.g., 'Contact', 'Deal'). Used in UI when referring to a single record. Must contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores, with no consecutive underscores, and cannot be empty.

plural_label.description: Plural display label for the module (e.g., 'Contacts', 'Deals'). Used in UI when referring to multiple records. Must contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores, with no consecutive underscores, and cannot be empty.

id.description: Unique identifier of the module to update. Must be a valid existing module ID.

profiles.description: Optional list of user profiles to add or remove from this module. Uses delta semantics: profiles without `_delete` are added (if not already present), profiles with `_delete: null` are removed. Omit this field to leave profile assignments unchanged. Each profile ID represents a permission set determining which users can view, create, edit, or delete records in this module.

200.description: Successfully updated all modules in the batch. All requested module updates completed without errors.

schema.description: Successful batch update response containing results for all updated modules. All operations completed successfully without errors.

modules.description: Array of success results for each updated module, preserving request order.

code.description: Success code indicating module was updated

details.description: Success details containing the updated module ID

207.description: Multi-Status - partial success. Returned when batch update contains both successful and failed module updates. Response includes individual status for each module in the request.

schema.description: Multi-status batch update response containing individual results for each module in the request. Results preserve request order and include both successful and failed updates.

modules.description: Array of per-module results preserving request order. Each element represents the outcome for one module update operation.

code.description: Success code indicating module was updated

details.description: Success details containing the updated module ID

400.description: Bad request - validation failure. Returned when input data fails validation (invalid module ID, unsupported field values, field length exceeded, missing required fields, or attempting unsupported operations on system modules). No modules are updated when this error occurs.

schema.description: Validation error response for batch update failures. Returned when all modules in the request fail validation. No modules are updated when this error occurs.

modules.description: Array of error results for each module in the request, preserving request order.

items.description: INVALID_MODULE error for invalid or non-existent module ID

title: Get module metadata by API name
description: Retrieves complete metadata for a specific CRM module identified by its API name. Returns comprehensive configuration including fields, layouts, profiles, related lists, custom views, and module capabilities.
purpose: Retrieves complete metadata for a specific CRM module identified by its API name. Returns comprehensive configuration including fields, layouts, profiles, related lists, custom views, and module capabilities.

parameter_details.name: moduleIdentifier
parameter_details.description: Identifier for the module to retrieve, either numeric ID or API name. Use numeric ID for precise identification or API name for human-readable reference. Case-sensitive when using API name.

200.description: Successfully retrieved module metadata. Returns complete module configuration including fields, layouts, profiles, related lists, and capabilities.

schema.description: Module metadata response containing complete configuration details for the requested CRM module.

modules.description: Array containing module metadata. Typically contains a single module object for GET by api_name.

items.description: Complete metadata for a CRM module including configuration, capabilities, fields, layouts, profiles, and related lists.

global_search_supported.description: Indicates whether this module supports global search functionality.

public_fields_configured.description: Indicates whether public fields have been configured for this module.

recycle_bin_on_delete.description: Indicates whether deleted records are moved to recycle bin instead of permanent deletion.

has_more_profiles.description: Indicates whether there are additional profiles beyond those returned in the response (pagination indicator).

sub_menu_available.description: Indicates whether this module has a submenu available in the UI.

lookupable.description: Indicates whether this module can be used as a lookup target by other modules.

profile_count.description: Number of profiles available for this module. 0 indicates module has no profile assignments. Maximum 203 profiles per module.

module_type.description: Classification of the module - ENTITIES for standard data modules, CONFIGURATION_ENTITIES for configuration/settings modules.

cc_enabled.description: Indicates whether CommandCenter functionality is enabled for this module.

deletable.description: Indicates whether records in this module can be deleted.

description.description: User-defined description of the module. Null when no description has been set; empty string not used.

source.description: Source or origin of the module configuration.

creatable.description: Indicates whether new records can be created in this module.

inventory_template_supported.description: Indicates whether this module supports inventory template functionality.

modified_time.description: Timestamp when the module configuration was last modified in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (e.g., 2025-11-20T14:30:00+05:30). Null for modules never modified after initial creation.

presence_sub_menu.description: Indicates whether presence submenu is available for this module.

triggers_supported.description: Indicates whether workflow triggers are supported for this module.

api_name.description: API name used to reference this module in API calls.

plural_label.description: Translated/localized plural display label for the module.

actual_plural_label.description: Database/system plural label (untranslated base value) for the module.

actual_singular_label.description: Database/system singular label (untranslated base value) for the module.

singular_label.description: Translated/localized singular display label for the module.

isBlueprintSupported.description: Indicates whether Blueprint (process automation) is supported for this module.

visibility.description: Bitwise feature visibility flags controlling module and feature visibility. Base value 1=VISIBLE (fully visible). Add flag values to hide features: 2=WEBUIHIDDEN, 4=TABMENU, 8=LOOKUP, 16=RELATEDLIST, 32=MULTISELECTLOOKUP, 64=MODULE_SETTING, 128=AUTOMATION, 256=TEMPLATES, 512=IMPORT, 1024=EXPORT, 4096=REPORT, 8192=API. Examples: 1=fully visible, 4612 (4+512+4096)=hides TabMenu+Import+Report, 14316=complex combination hiding multiple features.

convertable.description: Indicates whether records in this module can be converted to other module records.

editable.description: Indicates whether records in this module can be edited.

emailTemplate_support.description: Indicates whether email templates are supported for this module.

email_parser_supported.description: Indicates whether email parsing functionality is supported for this module.

filter_supported.description: Indicates whether filtering/custom views are supported for this module.

show_as_tab.description: Indicates whether this module is displayed as a tab in the UI.

web_link.description: Web link/URL associated with the module, if any.

viewable.description: Indicates whether records in this module can be viewed.

api_supported.description: Indicates whether this module is accessible via API.

quick_create.description: Indicates whether quick create functionality is available for this module.

generated_type.description: Type indicating how the module was generated - default for system modules, custom for user-created, subform for related subforms, etc.

static_subform_properties.description: Configuration for static subform fields within this module.

feeds_required.description: Indicates whether activity feeds are required/enabled for this module.

scoring_supported.description: Indicates whether lead/contact scoring is supported for this module.

webform_supported.description: Indicates whether web forms can be created for this module.

arguments.description: Array of argument objects used to substitute parameters in web tab URLs (e.g., https://...&id=${Users.User Id}&name=${Users.First Name}). Duplicates allowed; order preserved for multi-occurrence parameters. Empty for non-web-tab modules.

business_card_field_limit.description: Maximum number of fields that can be displayed in business card view for this module (non-negative).

access_type.description: Access control type for the module - org_based for organization-wide access or team_based for team-specific access.

private_profile.description: Profile configuration for team-based access control. Populated only when access_type is 'team_based' with profile name and ID. Null for 'org_based' access modules.

track_current_data.description: Indicates whether current data tracking is enabled for this module.

modified_by.description: User who last modified this custom view. Null for system views that have never been modified.

profiles.description: List of profiles with access to this module

parent_module.description: Parent module reference for child/related modules (subforms, field trackers). Empty object {} for standalone modules; populated with api_name and id for modules with parent relationships.

$field_states.description: Available field states for dynamic field visibility and validation.

business_card_fields.description: Fields displayed in business card view. Only exposed when 'business_card_fields' is included in the include parameter. Can be empty array, array of objects with api_name and id, or array of string API names.

per_page.description: Default number of records displayed per page for this module.

$on_demand_properties.description: Properties that can be loaded on-demand rather than by default.

search_layout_fields.description: Fields shown in search result layouts.

kanban_view_supported.description: Indicates whether kanban view is supported for this module.

lookup_field_properties.description: Properties and configuration for lookup field behavior in this module.

kanban_view.description: Indicates whether kanban view is enabled for this module.

chart_view.description: Indicates whether chart view is enabled for this module.

chart_view_supported.description: Indicates whether chart view is supported for this module.

related_lists.description: Configuration for related lists (subforms and related modules) displayed within this module's detail view. Only exposed when 'related_lists' is included in the include parameter.

filter_status.description: Indicates the current status of filters for this module.

related_list_properties.description: Configuration and settings for related lists in this module.

display_field.description: API name of the display field

layouts.description: List of layout configurations available for this module, ordered by relevance to profiles and record creation. Only exposed when 'layouts' is included in the include parameter.

fields.description: Complete field metadata for all fields in this module. Only exposed when 'fields' is included in the include parameter and the service is creator.

custom_view.description: Custom view configuration defining filters, fields, and display settings for the module. Only exposed when 'custom_view' is included in the include parameter.

zia_view.description: Indicates whether Zia AI view is enabled for this module. Only exposed for Deals module.

default_mapping_fields.description: Default field mappings for module data import and integration operations. Only exposed for SALESORDERS, INVOICES, QUOTES, QUOTEDITEMS, ORDEREDITEMS, INVOICEDITEMS modules.

activity_badge.description: Activity badge configuration status for the module.

status.description: Current visibility status of the module in the system.

sequence_number.description: Module display ordering index in navigation/module list. Lower values appear first. Range 1-200.

$others_awaiting.description: Indicates whether there are other users awaiting action on records in this module. Only exposed for Approvals module.

territory.description: Territory assignment for the module. Null when territory management is not enabled.

showleadchainsync.description: Indicates if lead chain synchronization is enabled for this module. Only exposed for Leads module.

show_social.description: Indicates if social tabs features are enabled for this module.

show_visitor.description: Indicates if ZLiveDesk feature is enabled for this module.

show_googlesync.description: Indicates if Google Contact Sync feature is enabled for this module.

showtiktoksync.description: Indicates if TikTok LeadChain Sync feature is enabled for this module.

show_webform.description: Indicates if web forms feature is enabled for this module.

showfacebooksync.description: Indicates if Facebook LeadChain Sync feature is enabled for this module.

show_emailparser.description: Indicates if Email Parser feature is enabled for this module.

showlinkedinsync.description: Indicates if LinkedIn LeadChain Sync feature is enabled for this module.

masked_fields_count.description: Number of fields that are masked in this module. only exposed when field masking feature is enabled.

204.description: No Content - The requested module is not available for retrieval via this API endpoint. This occurs when the module exists in the system but is not exposed through the single module GET API (e.g., web tab modules, certain system modules). The module may still be accessible through the bulk modules API (GET /crm/v8/settings/modules). No response body is returned.

400.description: Bad Request - Syntactic validation failure (malformed or empty api_name parameter) or semantic error (module exists but not supported in API, API version incompatibility). Includes structured error codes: NOT_SUPPORTED (module not supported/invalid), API_NOT_SUPPORTED (API version incompatible), INVALID_REQUEST (malformed parameters).

schema.description: NOT_SUPPORTED error when the module is not supported in the API.

code.description: Error code indicating the module is not supported.

resource_path_index.description: Index of the invalid resource in the URL path (0-based). Typically 2 for the {api_name} parameter.

message.description: Human-readable error message explaining that the module is not supported.

title: Update module labels and profiles
description: Partially updates a module's display labels (singular and plural) and associated profile permissions. This operation only modifies the specified fields without affecting other module configuration. The operation is idempotent - calling it multiple times with the same payload produces the same result.
purpose: Partially updates a module's display labels (singular and plural) and associated profile permissions. This operation only modifies the specified fields without affecting other module configuration. The operation is idempotent - calling it multiple times with the same payload produces the same result.

parameter_details.name: moduleIdentifier
parameter_details.description: Identifier for the module to retrieve, either numeric ID or API name. Use numeric ID for precise identification or API name for human-readable reference. Case-sensitive when using API name.

request_body.description: Module update payload containing the module ID, new labels, and profile assignments. Contains a single module in the array.

schema.description: Request payload for updating module configuration including labels and profile associations.

modules.description: Array containing a single module object to update.

items.description: Module configuration object containing labels and profile assignments to update.

singular_label.description: Singular display label for the module. Maximum 25 characters, alphanumeric and spaces only, no special characters allowed.

plural_label.description: Plural display label for the module. Maximum 25 characters, alphanumeric and spaces only, no special characters allowed.

id.description: Unique numeric identifier of the module to update. Must match an existing module. Numeric string representation of a 64-bit positive integer (Java long/DB bigint).

profiles.description: List of unique profile IDs that should have access to this module. Cannot be empty. Order not significant. Maximum 203 profiles allowed.

items.description: Profile assignment specifying which profile should have access to this module.

200.description: Module successfully updated. Returns the updated module ID and success confirmation.

schema.description: Response wrapper containing the result of the module update operation.

modules.description: Array containing a single success response object for the updated module.

items.description: Success response object containing operation result, module ID, and status.

code.description: Operation result code indicating successful update.

details.description: Details object containing the ID of the updated module.

message.description: Human-readable success message confirming the module update.

400.description: Bad Request - validation errors, invalid module, unsupported operation, or API version not supported. Returns specific error codes: INVALID_MODULE (module not found or path mismatch), INVALID_DATA (label exceeds 25 chars or contains special characters), API_NOT_SUPPORTED (API version incompatibility), MANDATORY_NOT_FOUND (mandatory field missing).

schema.description: INVALID_MODULE error when the module API name in the URL path is invalid or does not exist.

code.description: Error code indicating the module is invalid or not found.

details.description: Details about the invalid module reference.

resource_path_index.description: Index of the invalid resource in the URL path (0-based). Typically 2 for the {api_name} parameter.

message.description: Human-readable error message explaining the invalid module.

title: Retrieve pick list values for a field
description: Returns the available pick list values for a specified field in a module, including display values (with translations if enabled), reference values, layout associations, and metadata. Returns 204 if the field exists but is not a pick list type. Does not support pagination.
purpose: Returns the available pick list values for a specified field in a module, including display values (with translations if enabled), reference values, layout associations, and metadata. Returns 204 if the field exists but is not a pick list type. Does not support pagination.

parameter_details.name: fieldId
parameter_details.description: Unique numeric identifier for the field. Must be a valid field ID belonging to the specified module.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: API name of the CRM module containing the field. Supports standard modules (e.g., Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Deals) and custom modules. Case-insensitive.

200.description: Successfully retrieved pick list values with metadata including sequence, display values (translated if enabled), reference values, color codes, and layout associations.

schema.description: Response schema for pick list values with metadata

pick_list_values.description: Array of pick list value objects with metadata

sequence_number.description: Display order sequence for this pick list value

display_value.description: Translated value displayed to users (same as reference_value if translation not enabled)

reference_value.description: Untranslated reference value used for API operations

colour_code.description: Hexadecimal color code for UI rendering. Null if no color assigned.

actual_value.description: Internal value used for system operations

id.description: Unique identifier for this pick list value

type.description: Usage status of the pick list value

layout_associations.description: Layouts where this pick list value is available. Null if the value is unused or not associated with any layout. Layout IDs are unique within this array.

204.description: Field exists in the module but is not a pick list type; no content returned.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid field_id, invalid module name, or version not supported.

schema.description: Invalid field_id provided in path parameter

details.description: Additional context about the invalid field_id error

resource_path_index.description: Zero-based index of the invalid path parameter (2 indicates fieldId position)

403.description: Insufficient permissions to access field metadata; requires Crm_Implied_Customize_Zoho_CRM permission.

schema.description: Permission denied error response

details.description: Additional context about the permission error

permissions.description: Required permissions that are missing. Array contains unique permission identifiers; order is not significant.

message.description: Human-readable error message describing the permission denial

title: Get Record Count in a Module
description:
Fetches the total number of records in a specified module.
The count can be filtered using `cvid` (Custom View ID) or one of the search parameters (`criteria`, `phone`, `email`, `word`).

**Important Constraint (Zoho Documentation):**
You can only include **either** `cvid` **or** one of the search parameters (`criteria`, `phone`, `email`, `word`) in a single request. Combining `cvid` with any search parameter will result in an `AMBIGUITY_DURING_PROCESSING` error (HTTP 400).
purpose:
Fetches the total number of records in a specified module.
The count can be filtered using `cvid` (Custom View ID) or one of the search parameters (`criteria`, `phone`, `email`, `word`).

**Important Constraint (Zoho Documentation):**
You can only include **either** `cvid` **or** one of the search parameters (`criteria`, `phone`, `email`, `word`) in a single request. Combining `cvid` with any search parameter will result in an `AMBIGUITY_DURING_PROCESSING` error (HTTP 400).

parameter_details.name: moduleApiName
parameter_details.description: The API name of the module to retrieve the record count for.

parameter_details.name: email
parameter_details.description: Searches for records using an email address. The API searches across all email fields in the specified module and returns matching records. (e.g., `p%2Bboyle@abc.com` for `p+boyle@abc.com`).

parameter_details.name: phone
parameter_details.description: Searches for records using a phone number. The API searches across all phone fields in the specified module and returns matching records. (e.g., `9876543210`).

parameter_details.name: word
parameter_details.description: Performs a global search within the specified module using the given word. The matchable records across multiple fields based on the search term. (e.g., `fin` to match records containing 'fin' in any searchable field).

parameter_details.name: cvid
parameter_details.description: The ID of the custom view from which to get the record count.

parameter_details.name: criteria
parameter_details.description:
Performs search by following the shown criteria: `(({api_name}:{operator}:{value}) and/or ({api_name}:{operator}:{value}))`

Performs a search based on the following format:
`(({field_API_name}:{operator}:{value}) and/or ({field_API_name}:{operator}:{value}))`

Replace `{field_API_name}`, `{operator}`, and `{value}` with the appropriate field API name, condition, and value.

**Key Points:**
- You can search for a maximum of **10 criteria** (with same or different columns)
- The only operator that is supported for **encrypted fields** is `equals`
- When using the `equals` operator in the Search API, it behaves like `contains`, retrieving records that include the specified value

**Single Condition:**
If the condition is `(Company:equals:ABC)`, the response will include records with "ABC" as well as "ABC Inc" in the Company field.

**Multiple Conditions:**
`equals` still behaves like `contains`. For example, `((Company:equals:ABC) and (First_Name:starts_with:M))`, it retrieves records where the "First Name" starts with "M" and the "Company" contains "ABC" (e.g., "ABC" or "ABC Inc.").

**Note:** The above behaviour does not apply to the **picklist field type**.

**IN Operator:**
The `in` operator checks if a field's value matches any value in a given list.
For example, `(Full_Name:in:Patricia,Boyle,Kate)`, it retrieves records where the Full Name is Patricia, Boyle, or Kate.

**Special Character Handling:**
When a single-line field value contains characters such as `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `^`, `:`, `-`, `/`, `!`, `?`, `*`, `_`, `@`, space, the Search API returns records with similar-looking values, even if the characters are not an exact match.

For example, if Record A has the field value `sales-team@zoho.com` and Record B has `sales_team@zoho.com`, a search using `equals:sales-team@zoho.com` may return both records.

**Escaping Special Characters:**
When using parentheses `()`, commas `,`, or a backslash `\` as the last character in a search value, follow these steps:
1. Escape special characters using a backslash `\`
2. Encode the value before making the API request. Select the value of the criteria, right-click the value, and choose the EncodeURIComponent option.

**Example 1: Escaping Parentheses and Commas**
- Search term: `((Last_Name:equals:Burns,B) and (First_Name:starts_with:M))`
- Escape the comma `\,`: `((Last_Name:equals:Burns\,B) and (First_Name:starts_with:M))`
- Encode the value: `((Last_Name:equals:Burns%5C%2CB) and (First_Name:starts_with:M))`

**Example 2: Escaping a Backslash at the End**
- Search term: `(Last_Name:equals:K\)`
- Escape the backslash `\\`: `(Last_Name:equals:K\\)`
- Encode the value: `(Last_Name:equals:K%5C%5C)`

**Supported Data Types:**
`picklist`, `owner_lookup`, `user_lookup`, `lookup`, `phone`, `email`, `date`, `datetime`, `text`, `textarea`, `integer`, `currency`, `decimal`, `multiselectpicklist`, `bigint`, `percent`, `formula`, `website`, `boolean`, `double`

**Supported Operators:**
`equals`, `starts_with`, `in`, `not_equal`, `greater_equal`, `greater_than`, `less_equal`, `less_than`, `between`

title: Restore multiple recycle bin records
description: Restores multiple recycle-bin records based on provided criteria. You can specify records to restore using one of: `ids`, `filters`, or `restore_all_records`. Only one of these fields should be provided; supplying multiple will result in an error. When the number of records to restore (including child records) exceeds 1000, the operation is scheduled as a background job (202 status). For 1000 or fewer records, restoration happens immediately (200/207 status).

Only one of these fields should be provided; supplying multiple will result in an error. For 1000 or fewer records, restoration happens immediately (200/207 status).

group_operator.description: Logical operator for combining filter conditions. Only 'AND' is supported.

restore_all_records.description: If set to true, restores all records in the recycle bin. Mutually exclusive with `ids` and `filters`. If set to false, either `ids` or `filters` must be provided.

title: Empty the recycle bin
description: Permanently deletes all records from the Recycle Bin. This action is irreversible, as deleted records cannot be restored after this operation. When the number of records in the Recycle Bin exceeds 1000 (including child records), the deletion will be scheduled as a background job (202 status). If the count is 1000 or fewer, the records are deleted immediately (200 status). Only users with the Admin profile can empty the Recycle Bin.

title: GET /settings/automation/webhooks
description: Retrieve all available webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account.
purpose: Retrieve all available webhooks configured in your Zoho CRM account.

parameter_details.name: module
parameter_details.description: Represents the parent module that the webhook monitors

parameter_details.name: page
parameter_details.description: To get the list of webhooks from the respective pages. The default value is 1. Note that you cannot use this param with the "page_token" param.

parameter_details.name: per_page
parameter_details.description: Specify how many webhooks to return per page. The default and the maximum possible value is 200.

parameter_details.name: sort_by
parameter_details.description: To sort the webhooks based on the Modified_Time field.

parameter_details.name: sort_order
parameter_details.description: To sort the available list of webhooks in either ascending or descending order, based on the value of sort_by parameter.

parameter_details.name: feature_type

items.description: Alternate reorder item entry specifying a workflow rule ID and its new module-specific sequence index.

module_specific_sequence.description: Specify the new execution order for this Workflow rule within the module. The index starts from 1. (Required)
